title: astripomoea malvacea var. involuta
intro: Astripomoea malvacea var. involuta, also known as the invasive "apple of Perdition", is a plant species that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It is reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Astripomoea malvacea var. involuta is often used as a poultice or infested in teas, but it's crucial to note that its safety and effectiveness have not been extensively scientifically validated.
